2605.30322.title: Gram: Assessing sabotage propensities via automated alignment auditing
2605.30322.paper_summary: We introduce Gram, an automated alignment auditing framework to assess the propensity of AI agents to engage in sabotage. We evaluate Gemini models across 17 simulated agentic deployment scenarios that incentivize sabotage. We find Gemini models misbehave in about 2-3% of our simulated trajectories. Many of these cases are explained by "overeagerness" in Gemini models resulting in both excessive role-playing and goal-seeking behavior. In contrast to other alignment auditing approaches, Gram is designed to specifically evaluate misalignment and intentional sabotage in agentic coding and research agents. We additionally introduce an experimental investigator agent pipeline which enables fine-grained targeted experiments to identify the drivers of misbehavior. We find that increasing realism of environments and removing nudges to misbehave tends to reduce sabotage rates close to zero.
2605.30322.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹了Gram，一個自動化的對齊審計框架，用於評估AI代理參與破壞行為的傾向。<lb/>我們在17個模擬的代理部署場景中評估Gemini模型，這些場景激勵破壞行為。<lb/>我們發現Gemini模型在約2-3%的模擬軌跡中表現不當。<lb/>這些案例中的許多可以用Gemini模型的「過於熱心」來解釋，這導致了過度的角色扮演和目標追求行為。<lb/>與其他對齊審計方法相比，Gram專門設計用於評估代理編碼和研究代理中的不對齊和故意破壞行為。<lb/>我們還介紹了一個實驗調查代理管道，這使得能夠進行細緻的針對性實驗，以識別不當行為的驅動因素。<lb/>我們發現，提高環境的現實性並去除誘導不當行為的因素，往往能將破壞率降低到接近零。
2605.30322.author: David Lindner et.al.
2605.30322.authors: David Lindner, Victoria Krakovna, Sebastian Farquhar
